mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in recession risks within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during periods of low inflation confirms robust supply-side expansion, supporting corporate margins and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of low-inflation months across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings surpassing 3.5% trigger immediate Federal Reserve tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that eventually forces wage inflation, eroding corporate profits and causing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows and fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales during post-recession recovery phases confirms durable demand expansion, lifting equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP and retail sales combinations fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating short-term positive equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM changes yield negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year backtest period.
